aliases: South Indian Cashew Curry with Tandoori Roti
काजू कोरमा तंदूरी रोटी के साथ

english: South Indian Cashew Curry with Tandoori Roti
hindi: काजू कोरमा तंदूरी रोटी के साथ

food_subgroup: Curry with bread — cashew-based South Indian kurma served with tandoori flatbread

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cashew kurma with tandoori roti. FOODON_03301710 (curry dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Curry, meat or vegetable based, cooked dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (curry, cooked dish) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for cashew kurma or the combined meal with tandoori roti.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific South Indian cashew curry dish.

main_ingredients: aromatic spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf)

serving.description: 1 serving with 2 pieces of tandoori roti
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving consists of approximately 250 g of cashew kurma gravy accompanied by 2 pieces of tandoori roti (approximately 75 g each), totalling around 400 g.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient values estimated by component analysis: cashew-based vegetable kurma (ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017, item: Korma, vegetable) combined with tandoori roti (ICMR-NIN 2017, item: Roti, tandoori, whole wheat). Weighted blend at approximately 62.5% kurma gravy and 37.5% tandoori roti by weight.
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry covers this combined dish. Values are derived by weighted blending of ICMR-NIN 2017 entries for vegetable korma (cashew-enriched) and tandoori roti. Cashew kurma component is higher in fat due to cashew paste and cream. Per-100g values reflect the blended meal as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous foods: mixed vegetable curry with legumes/nuts (GI ~45–50) and tandoori roti/whole wheat flatbread (GI ~62), weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving. Whole wheat chapati GI reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008). International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Munthiri Kurma with Tandoori Roti. GI estimated as weighted average: tandoori roti (whole wheat flatbread, GI ~62, ~37.5% of serving carbohydrate) and cashew vegetable kurma (GI ~40 analogous to nut-enriched vegetable curry, ~62.5% of serving carbohydrate). Weighted GI ≈ 52. GL per serving = (52 × 94g carbohydrate in 400g serving) / 100 = 48.8, rounded to 48.6. gi_category set to low per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 1240 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a serving and request reduced salt preparation, as the combined sodium from the spiced kurma gravy and tandoori roti can contribute substantially to daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Estimated glycemic index of 52 (low) and glycemic load of approximately 48.6 per full serving; the high GL per serving reflects the large carbohydrate load from two pieces of tandoori roti.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this dish in moderated portions — one piece of tandoori roti instead of two — to reduce the glycemic load per meal, while benefiting from the low-GI cashew-based kurma which slows glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 3.1 g per 100 g from cream and cashew paste; a full serving provides approximately 12.4 g saturated fat, approaching the daily limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be aware that the cream and cashew content elevates saturated fat intake; requesting a lighter preparation with reduced cream and choosing one roti instead of two can meaningfully lower the saturated fat and caloric load per meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Munthiri Kurma with Tandoori Roti is a South Indian restaurant dish consisting of a rich, creamy cashew-based curry (kurma) served alongside two pieces of tandoori roti. The word 'munthiri' means cashew in Tamil and Malayalam, and the kurma is characterised by a smooth, ivory-coloured gravy made from blended cashew paste, onion, tomato, yogurt, and cream, tempered with whole spices including cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, and bay leaf. The flavour profile is mildly spiced, subtly sweet from the cashews, and aromatic. The tandoori roti is a whole-wheat flatbread baked in a clay tandoor oven, giving it a slightly charred exterior and a soft, chewy interior. Together, the dish provides a balanced combination of complex carbohydrates from the roti and healthy fats and plant protein from the cashew-enriched gravy. Nutritionally, the dish is moderately energy-dense at approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with notable fat content from cashews and cream (9.2 g fat per 100 g), moderate protein (5.8 g), and a significant carbohydrate contribution from the roti (23.5 g per 100 g). The estimated glycemic index of 52 places it in the low-GI category, though the glycemic load per full serving is high due to portion size. Clinically, sodium content warrants attention for hypertensive individuals, and portion control of the roti component is advisable for those managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk. This dish is vegetarian, halal-suitable, and commonly found in South Indian and Indian restaurant menus across Singapore.
common_user_queries: Calories in munthiri kurma with tandoori roti

ingredient_synonyms.kurma: korma — a mild, creamy spiced curry
ingredient_synonyms.tandoori roti: whole wheat flatbread baked in a clay tandoor oven
ingredient_synonyms.cashew paste: blended cashew nuts used as a thickening and flavouring base
ingredient_synonyms.korma: alternate spelling of kurma; mild spiced curry with cream or yogurt base

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry covers this exact combined dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component blending of ICMR-NIN 2017 entries for vegetable korma and tandoori roti. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because both component sources are primary approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the ICMR-NIN 2017 tables for the component foods used in this estimation. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for this dish. GI category is low (GI = 52, which is ≤ 55 per the validator threshold rule). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (52 × 94) / 100 = 48.88, rounded to 48.6, based on approximately 94 g total carbohydrate in a 400 g serving (23.5 g per 100 g × 4). The dish is served at Manam - Upper Circular Road on field survey data at SGD 15.00.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros present and numeric: energy_kcal 198.0, protein_g 5.8, fat_g 9.2, carbohydrate_g 23.5. Source is ICMR-NIN 2017 with transparent weighted component estimation. No zero macros.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 2.1, sodium_mg 310.0, saturated_fat_g 3.1, sugar_g 3.2 — all present as numeric values and plausible for this dish.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 52 (numeric, > 0), gi_category 'low' (correct per ≤ 55 threshold), glycemic_load_per_serving 48.6, gi_source present citing University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal. Analogous estimate basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (curry dish) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0CKP (curry, cooked dish) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT set to none with gap note. No codes invented. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate, human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.
